objective: Run the bounded signature support diagnostic at the full candidate-pair cap to determine whether rows_per_family=20 support is attainable.
non_negotiables: The result is a support diagnostic, not a materialized shard.
The report must preserve candidate offsets and cap-hit flags per topology family.
A positive support result must still require schema validation, split reports, floors, and projection inventories before model work.
Existing rows_per_family=10 artifacts remain the current materialized diagnostic shard until a new shard is generated and audited.

tasks.prompt: Use the Wave 42 bounded-support CLI at candidate-pair-limit 8280 to test whether rpf20 signature support is reachable without hitting the candidate cap.
acceptance: The report completes and records candidate offsets for all topology families.
The report states whether each topology family reaches the 20-row target.
The report states whether any topology family hit the 8280-pair cap.

tasks.prompt: Record the full-pair bounded support report, its hash, and the interpretation that rpf20 support is attainable but not yet materialized or split.
acceptance: The report records 60 selected rows, split 20/20/20 across the three topology families.
No topology family hits the 8280 candidate-pair cap.
Docs state that the next wave must materialize and audit the rpf20 heuristic shard before model claims.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_43_signature_full_pair_support.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 43 plan is internally consistent.
The full-pair bounded support report is valid JSON.
